commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: Paul put wickedness at the head of the list, because he thought that evil and covetousness depended on it. He then added malice, from which flows envy, murder, strife and deceit. After this he put malignity, which generates gossip and slander. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Being filled with all iniquity. He passeth to many other sins and crimes of the heathens. (Witham)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Notice how everything here is intensive— ”filled” and “with all.” Having named maliciousness in general, Paul goes on to discuss the particulars, and these too he mentions in excess—”full of envy,” etc.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Being filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, debate, deceit, malignity; whisperers, See how everything here is intensitive. For he says, being filled, and with all, and having named maliciousness in general, he also further pursues the particulars, and these too in excess, saying, Full of envy, murder, for the latter of these comes from the former, as was shown in Abel's case and Joseph's, and then after saying, debate, deceit, malignity,.
